blockingIssues.description: Reminder edit/delete mutations are applied to `first reminder whose name is ...` in AppleScript, so duplicate titles can mutate the wrong reminder and break ID-based endpoint semantics.
PUT/DELETE reminders endpoints resolve IDs from `reminders_list(..., limit=500)`, causing false 404 responses for valid reminder IDs outside the first 500 results.
After reminder list filter changes, `active_reminder` is not reset; complete/edit/delete actions can target a hidden previously-selected reminder rather than a currently visible selection.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require feature handoffs to set `commitId` to the implementation commit (or implementation range), not a follow-up docs/library-only commit.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The reminders-tui-tab handoff references commit 223bf9f, which only updates .factory/library/reminders-tab.md; the actual feature diff for inbox.py/tests is in 85b66ab.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that interactiveChecks must record executed validation actions and observed outcomes, not hypothetical 'would run' statements.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The reminders-tui-tab transcript/handoff used hypothetical interactive checks and had no tuistory execution evidence despite UI-heavy scope.

rejectedObservations.observation: Architecture library doc still says Reminders mutations are complete/create only.
